feed.title: Yasassri Ratnayake
feed.subtitle: A minimal, responsive, and powerful Jekyll theme for presenting professional writing.

entry.title: Create Jenkins Credentials Through Rest API

link.title: Create Jenkins Credentials Through Rest API

entry.summary: In this post I’ll explain how you can create Jenkins credentials through the Jenkins Rest API. Let’s get into it straight away. How To Authenticate Jenkins API Inorder to Authenticate the API call you have to pass an API token and the Jenkins Crump with the API call. Jenkins Crunb was introduced to prevent CSRF attacks. Let’s see how exactly you can get all these details. $JENKINS_URL : This...

entry.title: Role Based Authorization Hanlder for WSO2 Micro Integrator

link.title: Role Based Authorization Hanlder for WSO2 Micro Integrator

entry.summary: WSO2 Micro Integrator(MI) is a lightweight integration platform that allows you to write different integrations to connect systems just like an Enterprise Service Bus. If you are familiar with the WSO2 products stack, WSO2 MI is the descendent of Enterprise Integrator and MI will be eventually replacing EI. When creating different services that serve your enterprise traffic it’s important to k...

entry.title: How to use Windows Authentication for MSSQL with WSO2 Servers.

link.title: How to use Windows Authentication for MSSQL with WSO2 Servers.

entry.summary: Every WSO2 product require a DB to persist different data. If we take WSO2 API Manager there are multiple DBs used to store different sets of data, for example, APIM DB, Registry DB, User DB etc. By default these databases are pointed to a H2 Database which resides in the file system. For production deployments these databases should be externalised by pointing it to a production grade Database...

entry.title: Building Your WSO2 Integrations with Unit Tests; A Guide for WSO2 Micro Integrator & Enterprise…

link.title: Building Your WSO2 Integrations with Unit Tests; A Guide for WSO2 Micro Integrator & Enterprise…

entry.summary: WSO2 Micro Integrator(MI) and Enterprise Integrator(EI) is a feature rich Opensource Integration platform which provides traditional Enterprise Service Bus capabilities combined with modern cutting edge features. Developing and deploying integration use cases with WSO2 MI/EI is similar to developing any kind of application, which follows a similar lifecycle. When developing an integration use c...

entry.title: Keeping Your WSO2 Products up-to-date in a Containerised Environment

link.title: Keeping Your WSO2 Products up-to-date in a Containerised Environment

entry.summary: Modern day enterprises and businesses rely on a variety of integral applications/systems to fulfill their business requirements. These systems can vary from a simple HR system to a complex API Management platform. Given the businesses are highly dependent on these different underline systems that are integrated into their core business requirements, it’s crucial to keep these systems up to date...
